hero.h1: Let happy customers speak. Keep complaints private.
hero.lede: ReviewShield asks every customer for a rating. 4 or 5 stars goes straight to Google. A lower score comes directly to you — never posted online.

how.h2: Three steps, then it runs itself
how.sub: One line of code on the right page, and the rest is automatic.

step1.p: Place one line on the page where happy customers land — like your thank-you page after an appointment or order.

step2.p: The widget asks for a 1 to 5 star rating, in your own colors and style.

step3.p: 4 or 5 stars: straight to Google. Lower: feedback comes to you privately, no public review.

branches.eyebrow: For every sector with customer contact
branches.sub: Whether customers come for an appointment, a quote, an order or a job — the principle works the same everywhere.

examples.h2: In your brand colors, on any website
examples.sub: The widget adapts to your colors. Three examples in different brand styles below.

demo.sub: This is exactly how the widget appears on your customer's website. No real review is affected.

context.label: EXAMPLE PAGE
context.p: Imagine: a customer of Anna's Salon just had a haircut and lands on the thank-you page.

widget.q: How was your experience at Anna's Salon?
widget.hint: Click on a number of stars

redirect.title: Great to hear!
redirect.sub: You are now being redirected to Google to leave your review.

feedback.title: We'd love to make it right
feedback.sub: Your feedback goes directly and privately to the business. No public review will be posted.

feedbackthanks.title: Thank you for your feedback
feedbackthanks.sub: The business has been notified and may contact you.

roi.h2: What a better score is worth to you
roi.sub: Enter your current situation and see the impact of more positive reviews on your revenue.

explainer.default: Research on local search results shows that a higher Google score correlates with more inquiries. This is an estimate, not a guarantee.
explainer.maxed: Your score is already close to the maximum. The biggest benefit of ReviewShield is then preventing future negative reviews.

1.text: Within two months we went from 4.1 to 4.6 on Google. Complaints now reach me instead of going public.

2.text: Installation took me literally two minutes. No technical knowledge needed, and it works exactly as promised.

3.text: We now get notified much more often when a customer is unhappy, before it becomes a public review.

testimonials.h2: Business owners who already improved their score

1.p: Submitted complaints (1-3 stars) are forwarded directly and exclusively to you as the business owner via email. They are not stored on our servers and not shared with third parties.

2.p: The widget does not ask for your end customer's name or email address. Only the star rating and submitted text are forwarded. The customer remains anonymous if they wish.

3.p: Your name, email address and payment details are managed via Stripe, a certified payment processor. We never have access to your payment card details.

privacy.sub: Transparency about data processing matters for both you as a business and your end customers. Here's how it works.

compare.h2: Why this is cheaper and simpler
compare.sub: Reputation management tools have existed for a while — usually priced and built for large companies.

pricing.sub: Start with one platform, or cover everything at once. Cancel monthly.

lokaal.name: Local
lokaal.sub: For those just starting with review management

pro.name: Professional
pro.sub: For those who want to be everywhere
pro.f2: Full brand styling: color, logo, font

urgency.h2: Every month you wait costs you something
urgency.sub: Unanswered negative reviews and missed positive reviews accumulate, even when you're not looking.
urgency.stat1: of consumers read online reviews of a local business before visiting or contacting them.
urgency.stat2: only choose a business with four or more stars. Below 4 stars, more and more potential customers will skip you.
urgency.stat3: free to experience it yourself. The sooner you start, the sooner satisfied customers become visible.
urgency.source: Sources: Capital One Shopping Research (2025-2026), BrightLocal Local Consumer Review Survey (2026)

exit.h: Not ready for a subscription yet?
exit.p: Leave your email and receive a free guide: 5 ways to get more reviews.
exit.confirm: Thanks! The guide is on its way to your inbox.

signup.sub: A few short steps. At the end, your own widget code lands in your inbox.

conf.sub: We've also sent everything to your email.

opt1.p: Place this code on the page where happy customers arrive. The widget appears automatically as a pop-up.

opt2.p: This shows the widget permanently in a fixed place on your page — quieter than a pop-up, ideal for a contact page or footer.

howpage.sub: Everything you need to get the widget live on your website.

s1.p: The widget works best on a page people see after a positive experience: a thank-you page after an order, a confirmation page after an appointment, or an email after completing a job.

s2.h: 2. Paste the code into your website
s2.p: Open your website's page editor (WordPress, Shopify, Wix, or your own HTML file) and paste the code just before the closing </body> tag, or in an HTML block in the desired location.

s3.p: Open your page in an incognito window and click through the stars. With 4 or 5 stars you get redirected to your review link; with a lower score you see the internal feedback form.

s4.p: The pop-up code makes the widget appear automatically after a few seconds on the page. The embedded block shows the widget permanently in a fixed spot — useful if you want it visible rather than as an overlay.

s5.h: 5. What if a customer closes the pop-up?
s5.p: No problem. Whoever closes it simply hasn't given a rating — that's always their choice. The widget never intervenes if someone doesn't want to participate. Customers without a smartphone don't need to do anything; the widget is completely optional.

faqpage.sub: Can't find your question? Send an email to support{'@'}reviewshield.nl.

contactpage.sub: Have a question, want to report something, or stuck somewhere? We respond within one business day.

response.p: We respond within one business day. Installation questions are often resolved the same day.

opzeg.p: Cancel by sending a simple email to cancel{'@'}reviewshield.nl. No hassle, no forms.
